titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/158

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=435 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:19:30.413204+00 | translation moa=436 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:19:28.35207+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Gaufridus filius Walleui de lilliscliue Salutem . Sciatis me dedisse et concessisse
et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte marie de Melros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus in liberam . puram . et perpetuam ele
elemosinam quatuor Bouatas terre arabilis . In territorio de Wittun . sicut iacent in uno tenemento desuper Rauenesfen . propinqui
ores terre eorundem monachorum quam habent ex donacione patricii de Ridale domini mei . Totam hanc terram et quicquid
continetur infra diuisas predicte terre dedi predictis monachis de Melros libera . quieta . et soluta ab omni terreno seruicio et consue
tudine . et exactione seculari . ut ea habent . teneant . et possideant in perpetuum ita libere . plene . et honorifice . sicut aliqua
elemosina liberius . quietius . plenius . et honorificentius teneri potest . et possideri . Et ego et heredes mei manutenebimus . et waranti
zabimus prefatis monachis eandem terram contra omnes homines . et adquietabimus de omni forensi seruicio . erga dominos
nostros . et de omnibus aliis rebus que pro aliqua terra exigi poterunt in perpetuum . Hiis . Test’. Roberto Glasguensi . archidiacono .
Patricio de Ridale domino meo . Waltero et Nichol’ filiis eius . Ricardo nano . Radulfo . de Ridale . Ricardo persona de Alnecrum . Roberto
de Bernoldebi . Adam de Wittun . Willelmo . de Wittun . Adam de Chattun . et aliis Multis .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future, Geoffrey son of Waldef of Lilliesleaf sends greeting.

Know me to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, four oxgangs of arable land in the territory of Whitton, just as they lie in one holding above ‘Raven’s Fen’, near the land of the same monks which they have from the donation of Patrick of Ryedale my lord; this whole land and whatever is comprised within the said boundaries of the land I have given to the said monks of Melrose free, quit, and loosed from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction, that they may have, hold, and possess them for ever as freely, fully and honourably as any alms can be freely, quitely, fully and honourably held and possessed. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the said monks the same land against all men, and discharge for ever all forinsec service towards our lords, and all other things which can be exacted for any land.

With these witnesses: Robert, archdeacon of Glasgow; Patrick of Ryedale my lord; Walter and Nicholas, his sons; Richard le Naym; Ralph of Ryedale; Richard, parson of Ancrum; Robert of Barnoldby; Adam of Whitton; William of Whitton; Adam of Chatto; and many others.
